input_text:
Added a compound index on (tenant_id, created_at DESC) to the events table.

The slow queries were all doing tenant-scoped event fetches ordered by time — something like "give me the last 100 events for tenant X." Without the index, PostgreSQL was doing a full table scan and sort. The table has 40M rows.

With the compound index, PostgreSQL can use a single index scan to both filter by tenant and return results in order. Query time dropped from 4.2 seconds to 18 milliseconds in our test environment.
reviewer_notes: Pure performance optimization — adding an index for an obvious slow query pattern. No architectural choice between alternatives is recorded.
